name: social-autoposter
description: Draft, review, approve, and autopilot X/Twitter posts. Thin desktop client over the social-autoposter pipeline.
long_description: A guided X/Twitter posting assistant. Run setup once to tell it your website, what you do, who to target, and your brand voice. Then draft_cycle scans X, drafts replies, and walks you through each one (approve or skip) before anything is posted. Flip to autopilot when you trust it, and check get_stats to see how your posts perform. This is a thin client: it shells out to the social-autoposter pipeline scripts in a repo clone on your machine, so it needs SAPS_REPO_DIR to point at that clone.

tools.name: setup
tools.description: Run first. Configures the single project this install posts for: website, description, target audience, brand voice.

tools.name: draft_cycle
tools.description: Scan X, draft replies locally, walk through each one (approve or skip), post only the approved ones.

tools.name: autopilot
tools.description: Enable or disable background X/Twitter posting (the launchd cycle).

tools.name: get_stats
tools.description: Read-only post and engagement stats for the X/Twitter rail over the last N days.

saps_repo_dir.title: Social Autoposter repo path
saps_repo_dir.description: Absolute path to your social-autoposter repo clone (the folder that contains config.json, scripts/, skill/). The MCP shells out to the pipeline scripts in this folder.

saps_python.title: Python interpreter
saps_python.description: Python used by the pipeline (must have psycopg2 etc). Leave as default unless you know you need a different one.
